evidence_scope: controlled public Chrome iPhone USB active handover
primary_result: retry0 failed 3/3; retry1 succeeded 3/3 after one failed first attempt
cm_evidence: No single-session browser CM; one retry1 row had qlog path validation but Chrome used two sessions
application_recovery_evidence: Strong: explicit app retry converted repeated failure into success
paper_use: Strongest user-task continuity case; upload failure maps directly to task failure
next_required_experiment: Repeat with page-ready trigger if possible; compare resumable/multipart upload semantics

evidence_scope: controlled public Chrome iPhone USB active handover plus local Range resume control
primary_result: timeout-only retry0 failed 3/3; timeout+retry1 succeeded 3/3; local Range 6000ms no-retry 1/3 PASS and retry2 3/3 PASS
cm_evidence: No single-session browser CM; Range retry rows used multiple Chrome QUIC sessions
application_recovery_evidence: Strong: app stream timeout/full retry and byte-range retry can restore task completion
paper_use: Shows HTTP/3 alone did not preserve long response, while app recovery can be full-retry or resumable-range recovery
next_required_experiment: Run public page-ready Range handover after controlled origin is reachable

evidence_scope: controlled public Chrome iPhone USB active handover plus local controls
primary_result: one valid no-retry public row failed after two poll requests; retry public rows invalid until page-ready runner
cm_evidence: No qlog path validation in valid public failure row
application_recovery_evidence: Incomplete public retry evidence; local controls show polling can complete or fail depending outage window/session churn
paper_use: Useful for dashboard/workflow freshness but requires page-ready public rerun
next_required_experiment: Run page-ready no-retry and retry2 polling after the controlled origin is reachable

evidence_scope: local Chrome UDP rebinding transient return-path pilot plus 3x replication and buffered playback control
primary_result: segment replication 3000ms/6000ms completed 3/3; buffered playback 3000ms completed 12/12 but low buffer had 14 rebuffer events while high buffer had ~15s startup delay and 0 rebuffer
cm_evidence: Not single-session CM; every buffered playback row classified nat_rebinding_multiple_quic_sessions
application_recovery_evidence: High visible continuity, but quality-of-experience shifts between startup delay and rebuffering
paper_use: Shows streaming continuity can be preserved while hiding transport CM gaps, and must be measured with startup/rebuffer metrics
next_required_experiment: Run public page-ready buffered-media handover after controlled origin is reachable

evidence_scope: local Chrome UDP rebinding retry control
primary_result: 6000ms no-retry failed 3/3 after first segment; retry1 completed 3/3 with all eight segments
cm_evidence: Not single-session CM; retry1 rows used three Chrome QUIC sessions and no qlog path validation
application_recovery_evidence: Strong: one segment retry converted repeated failure into visible completion
paper_use: Use as application-recovery control showing streaming continuity depends on retry/buffer policy
next_required_experiment: Run public page-ready media handover after the controlled origin is reachable; add larger buffer-depth model if media section becomes central
